item_a.name: King Size Mattress
item_a.image_alt: King size mattress showing dimensions of 76 inches wide by 80 inches long
pros: Fits in most master bedrooms without overwhelming the space
Wide selection of bedding and accessories readily available
More affordable than super king options
Easier to move through doorways and staircases
cons: Less personal space per person compared to super king
May feel cramped for couples over 6 feet tall
Limited room for children or pets joining the bed
item_a.best_for: Couples with average-sized master bedrooms who want spacious sleeping without dominating the entire room

item_b.name: Super King Size Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Super king size mattress showing extra-wide dimensions of 72 inches wide by 84 inches long or 80 inches wide by 98 inches long
pros: Maximum sleeping space with 38-40 inches per person
Ideal for taller individuals with extra length
Accommodates children, pets, or restless sleepers comfortably
cons: Requires a large master bedroom (minimum 12x12 feet recommended)
Bedding is more expensive and harder to find
Higher overall cost for mattress and foundation
item_b.best_for: Couples with spacious master bedrooms who prioritize maximum comfort and personal sleeping space

comparison_summary: King mattresses at 76x80 inches work beautifully for most couples in standard bedrooms and won't break the bank. Super king sizes (typically 72x84 or 80x98 inches) offer incredible space but demand larger rooms, higher upfront costs, and pricier bedding forever.
verdict_text: King wins for practical buyers with standard bedrooms, but super king is unbeatable if you've got the space and budget for maximum sleeping luxury.

key_differences.item_b: 72 inches wide × 84 inches long (California King) or 80×98 inches (Texas King)

key_differences.item_a: 38 inches per person (equivalent to a crib width)
key_differences.item_b: 36-40 inches per person depending on variant
13 feet × 13 feet minimum for comfortable movement

key_differences.item_a: Widely available in all stores and online retailers
key_differences.item_b: Limited selection, often requires specialty orders

key_differences.item_a: Higher demand in secondary market, easier to sell
key_differences.item_b: Limited buyer pool, may be harder to resell

seo_content:
I've been testing mattresses for years, and the king vs super king question comes up constantly. Here's what you actually need to know.

The standard king mattress measures 76 inches wide by 80 inches long. It's been the go-to choice for couples for good reason—each person gets about 38 inches of personal space, which is basically like having your own twin bed. This works brilliantly for most couples who want spacious sleep without turning their bedroom into a furniture showroom. You'll need a room that's at least 12x12 feet, which leaves enough space for nightstands and walking around without bumping into things. The best part? Selection is massive. You can find king mattresses everywhere, from $500 budget options up to $3,000+ luxury models, and bedding is easy to source at any price point.

Super king mattresses are a different beast entirely. In the US, this usually means California King at 72x84 inches or the rare Texas King at 80x98 inches. These are spectacular for specific situations but come with real drawbacks you need to consider.

First, room size matters more than people think. You'll want at least 13x13 feet to avoid that cramped feeling where the bed swallows the entire space. The extra room is transformative if you're over 6'2" tall, if you and your partner are both restless sleepers, or if kids and pets regularly pile into your bed. You get roughly 40 inches per person, which dramatically cuts down on those middle-of-the-night disturbances.

But here's where it gets expensive. Bedding costs jump 30-50% higher than king size. Quality sheets run $80-$250 versus $50-$150 for king. Selection drops off sharply too—you're often shopping online at specialty retailers instead of picking something up at your local store. Moving these monsters is genuinely challenging. Standard doorways and staircases become obstacles, and you'll probably need professional movers. I've seen situations where upstairs installation required removing windows.

For most people, king mattresses hit the sweet spot. You get luxurious space without the headaches and premium costs of super king sizes. That said, if you've got a genuinely spacious master suite and the budget flexibility, a super king delivers an unmatched sleeping experience.

My advice? Measure your bedroom carefully. Don't just think about mattress dimensions—factor in the clearance you need to move comfortably around the room. That'll tell you what actually makes sense for your space.

faq.q: What is the actual size difference between king and super king mattresses?
faq.a: Standard king is 76×80 inches. Super king varies—California King is 72×84 inches (narrower but longer), while Texas King is 80×98 inches (wider and much longer). In the UK, super king measures 72×78 inches versus king at 60×78 inches. Different markets use these terms differently, so always check the actual dimensions.

faq.q: Will king size sheets fit a super king mattress?
faq.a: No, they won't fit properly. The size differences mean king sheets will be too small—they'll pull off the corners or won't cover the depth right. You need to buy sheets specifically made for your exact mattress size. This is one of those ongoing costs people forget about when upgrading.

faq.q: How much more expensive is a super king mattress compared to king?
faq.a: Expect to pay 40-60% more for a super king. They typically run $800-$4500 compared to $500-$3000 for king size. And that's just the mattress—you'll also pay 30-50% more for sheets, comforters, and mattress protectors for as long as you own it.

faq.q: What bedroom size do I need for a super king mattress?
faq.a: You want at least 13×13 feet (169 square feet) for a super king to feel comfortable. That gives you about 24 inches of clearance on each side so you're not squeezing past the bed constantly. King mattresses fit nicely in 12×12 foot rooms, which is why they work in more homes.

faq.q: Is a super king worth it for couples?
faq.a: It's worth it if your bedroom is larger than 13×13 feet and either of you is over 6'2" tall, you're both restless sleepers, or kids and pets regularly join you. For average-height couples in typical bedrooms, a king gives you plenty of space with better value and way easier bedding shopping.

faq.q: Is King Size Mattress better than Super King Size Mattress?
faq.a: It depends on your priorities. King mattresses are better for practical buyers with standard bedrooms and tighter budgets, offering excellent comfort for most couples without the premium price tag. Super king mattresses are better if you prioritize maximum sleeping space and have the room and budget to support them.

faq.q: Should I buy King Size Mattress or Super King Size Mattress?
faq.a: Buy a King if you have a standard bedroom, want to keep costs reasonable, and value practicality over excess space. Choose a Super King only if you have a large master bedroom, don't mind spending significantly more upfront and on bedding, and want maximum sleeping luxury.

faq.q: What is the difference between King Size Mattress and Super King Size Mattress?
faq.a: King mattresses measure 76x80 inches, while Super King mattresses are typically 72x84 or 80x98 inches, giving you considerably more width and length. Super Kings demand larger rooms, cost significantly more to purchase, and require premium-priced sheets and accessories that you'll replace throughout the mattress's life.
